name: prompts
context: Expose reusable prompt templates via MCP — structured message sequences with arguments, discovered via prompts/list
info: MCP prompts are user-controlled reusable templates that servers expose to structure interactions with language models. Unlike tools (model-controlled) and resources (application-controlled), prompts are designed for explicit user selection — typically surfaced as slash commands or menu items. Each prompt has a name, optional description, and optional arguments for customization. Prompts return an array of messages with roles (user/assistant) and content (text, images, audio, embedded resources). Prompts are discovered via `prompts/list` and retrieved via `prompts/get`. Servers declare the `prompts` capability during initialization with optional `listChanged: true` for dynamic prompt lists.
triggers: add a prompt template to my MCP server

sections.title: Prompt definition and arguments
sections.body: Each prompt requires a `name` and optionally has `title`, `description`, and `arguments`. Arguments have `name`, `description`, and `required` fields. In Python FastMCP, arguments are inferred from function parameters — required parameters become required arguments, parameters with defaults become optional. In TypeScript, use Zod schemas for argument validation.

sections.title: Prompt message structure
sections.body: Prompts return an array of messages, each with a `role` (user or assistant) and `content`. Content can be text, images, audio, or embedded resources. Multi-turn prompts use alternating user/assistant messages to set up conversation context — useful for few-shot examples or establishing personas.

sections.title: User interaction patterns
sections.body: Prompts are user-controlled — they should be explicitly selected by users, not automatically invoked by the model. Common UI patterns: slash commands (e.g., `/code_review`), command palette entries, context menu items, or quick action buttons. The MCP protocol doesn't mandate a specific UI pattern — host applications choose how to present prompts.
